title: Creamy Halloumi & Roasted Veg Burritos
summary: Take your Creamy Cauli & Halloumi Curry batch up a level with these easy burritos. Stuffed with roasted veg and zingy lime rice – they’re a perfect weeknight dinner or on-the-go lunch.

method.description: Before you begin, make the Creamy Cauli & Halloumi Curry batch recipe.

Heat the oven to 200°C. Slice the peppers into strips and peel and quarter the red onion. Toss them on a baking tray with a drizzle of olive oil, smoked paprika and a good pinch of salt. Roast for 20–25 mins until soft and slightly charred.

Cook the rice following pack instructions. Meanwhile, roughly chop the coriander and zest the lime. Once the rice is done, stir through the lime zest, chopped coriander and a squeeze of lime juice. Taste and season with salt.
Reheat the Creamy Cauli & Halloumi Curry gently in a pan or microwave until hot and spoonable. Add a splash of water if it needs loosening.
Warm the tortillas in a dry frying pan or microwave for 10–15 seconds so they’re soft and pliable.
To build, lay each tortilla flat and add a scoop of lime rice. Spoon over the creamy halloumi, then pile on the roasted veg. Add hot sauce or chilli flakes, if using.
Fold the sides in, roll up tightly from the bottom and wrap into a burrito. Serve straight away.

chefs.name: Jodie Nixon
chefs.summary: Jodie is a recipe writer, Mob food producer, and professional snacker. When she's not recreating all your childhood classics, she's creating delicious veg-forward dishes that'll prove you don't always need meat to have a good time. She's one hell of a talented cook.
